data.content: I see the issue. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the `.cs` file to disk but **never adds it to the `Compile` item group**, so the compiler doesn't know about it. The fix is to include the generated file in compilation and register it with `FileWrites` for clean support.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk but the compiler doesn't know about it. The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, after writing it.

This is a classic MSBuild anti-pattern: generating a source file during the build but not including it in the compilation items.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler didn't know about it.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target (after writing the file) with:
- `<Compile Include="...">` — tells the compiler to include the generated file
- `<FileWrites Include="...">` — registers it so `dotnet clean` removes it
